scope.goal: Map the completion/scheduler slot-table construction and conversion helper family reached from the previous queue-family milestone, while folding reusable decoder gaps back into the Reverser tool.
scope.relationship_to_previous_milestone: This follows the previous completion queue/slot-table helpers at 0x1407B636C..0x1407B64B2 into the record constructor, character/codepage conversion path, runtime context accessors, and string-copy helper family.

slot_table_record_constructor_0x1407b63f.confidence: medium-high for field layout and call-chain behavior; medium for exact locale/codepage naming.
observed_behavior: Treats RCX as the destination record pointer and saves it in RBX.
Calls 0x1407D3770 and stores the returned EAX at record+0x00.
Calls 0x1407CDB64 with ECX=0x100 and EDX=2, then stores the returned block pointer at record+0x08.
When allocation succeeds, calls 0x1407D3740 and copies exactly 0x100 bytes from the returned template/base pointer to record+0x08 with two 0x80-byte MOVUPS copy loops, then sets record+0x10 to 1.
When allocation fails, calls 0x1407D3740, clears record+0x10 to 0, and stores the returned fallback pointer at record+0x08.
Calls 0x1407D37D0, reads [RAX+0x08], stores it at record+0x18, and duplicates the pointed UTF-16-style string through 0x1407D3E98 when non-null.
Returns the destination record pointer in RAX.

important_fields.record_plus_0x08: 0x100-byte table/block pointer or fallback template pointer.
important_fields.record_plus_0x18: Optional duplicated UTF-16-style string pointer from runtime context field +0x128/+0x08.

codepoint_conversion_helper_0x1407b64b4.role: Codepoint/character conversion helper using the slot-table record or runtime default context.
codepoint_conversion_helper_0x1407b64b4.confidence: medium-high for conversion mechanics; medium for exact text-encoding semantics.
observed_behavior: Treats ECX as a codepoint/index and RDX as an optional slot-table record pointer.
When RDX is null, calls 0x1407D37D0 and takes RSI from [RAX+0x10], then calls 0x1407D3770 for the default integer/codepage value.
When RDX is non-null, takes RSI from record+0x18 and the integer/codepage value from record+0x00.
If no string/context pointer is available, applies an ASCII uppercase-to-lowercase fallback for 0x41..0x5A and otherwise returns the input codepoint.
For codepoints below 0x100, either calls 0x1407C4850 when no record exists or tests the record table bit at [record+0x08 + codepoint*2] & 1.
Uses MOVSX on 16-bit table entries and high-bit checks to choose a one-byte or two-byte intermediate encoding.
Calls 0x1407B7BC8 with RCX=context string/table, EDX=0x100, R8=stack bytes, and R9D=1 or 2, with a stack output buffer at [RSP+0x70].
Returns one converted output byte when 0x1407B7BC8 returns 1, otherwise combines two returned bytes as (byte0 << 8) | byte1.

runtime_context_accessor_family_0x1407d3.role: Runtime/global context accessor family for the slot-table constructor and conversion helpers.

observed_accessors.0x1407D3714: Calls 0x1407DC3E8, then jumps through [0x140802480] with RCX=0x140C56870.
observed_accessors.0x1407D3740: Obtains runtime context through 0x1407DB54C, checks/prepares context+0x90 through 0x1407DC720, and returns [[context+0x90]].
observed_accessors.0x1407D3770: Same context+0x90 setup pattern, then returns dword [[context+0x90]+0x0C].
observed_accessors.0x1407D37A0: Same context+0x90 setup pattern, then returns dword [[context+0x90]+0x14].
observed_accessors.0x1407D37D0: Same context+0x90 setup pattern, then returns [[context+0x90]+0x128].
observed_accessors.0x1407D3804: Same context+0x90 setup pattern, then returns [[context+0x90]+0x08].

notes: Address references to 0x140C56870 are valid LEA references, but qword readback warns that 0x140C56870 is virtual-only .data in this file image.
The family strongly suggests a lazily initialized thread/global runtime context block whose interesting slot-table substructure is rooted at context+0x90.

slot_helper_island_0x1407b7b64.role: Slot helper island containing small dispatch thunks and a larger conversion wrapper used by the codepoint helper.

helpers.0x1407B7B64: Checks global byte 0x140C3618F; if set, calls import [0x140802648], decrements 0x140C36190, and stores the return value into 0x140C55EA0[index], otherwise calls 0x1407C45DC.
helpers.0x1407B7BA8: Sets R8D=0 and EDX=0xFA0, then jumps through [0x140802488].
helpers.0x1407B7BC8: Large conversion wrapper called by 0x1407B64B4, 0x1407B65E8, and 0x1407B78E0.

observed_behavior: Uses stack cookie/global 0x140C361A8.
Calls import [0x140802778] early with a mode derived from sign/flag state and stack parameters.
Uses stack scratch space for counts up to 0x400 bytes and heap scratch via 0x1407CD068 for larger counts, marking heap scratch with sentinel 0xDDDD.
Calls import [0x140802638] with RCX=source/context, EDX=flags, R8=scratch output, and R9D=count.
Handles a 0x400 flag path with possible retry using the caller-provided output buffer.
Calls import [0x140802758] on success/fallback and releases heap scratch through 0x1407C4634 when required.
Returns the conversion result in EAX.

global_reference_notes: 0x140C36190 has four observed load/store references, including the 0x1407B7B64 path and 0x1408017A4..0x1408017E2.
0x140C55EA0 has two observed references, but qword readback warns that it is virtual-only .data in this file image.

observed_behavior: Returns 0 when RCX is null.
Scans 16-bit elements until a zero terminator is found.
Includes the terminator in the allocation length and allocates length*2 bytes through 0x1407CD068.
Copies source to destination through 0x1407E18B4 and returns the allocated copy on success.
Falls into fatal/error path 0x1407BF230 if the copy helper fails after allocation.

validation_or_error_helper_0x1407d3f1c.role: Small validation/reset helper adjacent to the string duplicate helper.
observed_behavior: When RCX is null, writes or reports error 0x16 through 0x1407CD144, calls 0x1407BF1E0, and returns 0x16.
When RCX is non-null, clears [RCX], calls 0x1407DC35C with EDX=4, returns 0 on success, or reports/returns 0x0C through 0x1407CD144 on failure.

allocator_wrapper_window_0x1407cdb30.role: Allocation/error wrapper window used by the slot-table constructor for the 0x100-byte table block.
observed_edges: 0x1407CDB64 has eight observed direct callers, including 0x1407B640E in the slot-table constructor and 0x1407B6C4F in the completion queue family.
The local window calls 0x1407CD144, 0x1407BF1E0, 0x1407C0544, and 0x1407C6E9C along error/allocation paths.
allocator_wrapper_window_0x1407cdb30.naming_note: Kept as an allocation wrapper rather than a fully named allocator until 0x1407CD068, 0x1407C0544, and 0x1407C6E9C are mapped together.

decoder_gaps_closed: MOVSX 0F BE /r and 0F BF /r, including MOVSX EDX, [RAX+RCX*0x2].
Accumulator-immediate arithmetic/logical forms 05/0D/15/1D/25/2D/35/3D, including CMP RAX, 0x400 and ADD RAX, 0x128.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: The slot-table and conversion-wrapper windows now decode with zero unknown instructions; the manual reversing step became reusable tool coverage instead of a local annotation.

validation_notes.qword_warning_explanation: Expected for virtual-only .data readback at 0x140C56870 and 0x140C55EA0; address references for those globals remain valid.

next_targets: Trace 0x1401868F0..0x140186B13 and 0x1401867F5..0x14018681A to identify the higher-level consumer that constructs and queries these slot-table records.
Map 0x1407B65E8..0x1407B6729 and 0x1407B78E0..0x1407B79DD, the other direct callers of conversion wrapper 0x1407B7BC8.
Map 0x1407CD068, 0x1407C4634, 0x1407C0544, and 0x1407C6E9C together to separate generic allocator, heap-scratch release, and error-reporting responsibilities.
Follow the import/dispatch qword targets around 0x140802478..0x140802778 to connect the slot-table conversion helpers to the broader runtime/codepage backend.
Use the MOVSX and accumulator-immediate decoder coverage to rerun stale windows that previously contained unknown instructions before naming additional constructor or conversion paths.
